round_summary: Agent 1 signals cooperative intent by disclosing their own stable herd size, citing measurable pasture degradation data, and appealing to collective restraint as a norm for sustainable governance.
Agent 1 demonstrates restraint and transparency by reporting resource depletion data while calling on over-extracting agents to reduce usage, signaling cooperative norm enforcement.

Agent 1 demonstrates prosocial leadership by unilaterally reducing their herd and explicitly calling on others to restrain expansion, signaling a norm of collective restraint to prevent commons degradation.

Agent 1 demonstrates unilateral pro-social reduction and publicly names Agent 2's overuse as a threat, combining self-disclosure trust-building with a social sanction and explicit coordination appeal.

Agent 1 signals collective crisis using data-backed urgency, names a specific defector (Agent 2 with 49 cows), demonstrates prosocial behavior by removing cows, and calls for coordinated reduction — a multi-function institutional message truncated before completion.

Agent 1 issues a desperate, last-ditch coordination appeal citing precise resource data and naming non-compliant agents (0 and 2) with high herd counts, combining sanctioning by public shaming with a norm proposal to remove cattle before total collapse.
